options.description: number the non-blank output lines, starting at 1
display non-printing characters (see the -v option), and display a dollar sign (‘$’) at the end of each line
set an exclusive advisory lock on the standard output file descriptor. This lock is set using fcntl(2) with the F_SETLKW command. If the output file is already
number the output lines, starting at 1
squeeze multiple adjacent empty lines, causing the output to be single spaced
display non-printing characters (see the -v option), and display tab characters as ‘^I’
display non-printing characters so they are visible. Control characters print as ‘^X’ for control-X; the delete character (octal 0177) prints as ‘^?’. Non-ASCII
